batch

Class MetadataScanner

  • java.lang.Object
    • batch.MetadataScanner
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.Iterable<MediaRecord>

    public class MetadataScanner
extends java.lang.Object
implements java.lang.Iterable<MediaRecord>

    • Constructor Detail

      • MetadataScanner

        protected MetadataScanner(BatchConfiguration settings)
        Constructs a new Executor using the specified configuration.
        Parameters:
        settings - the configuration settings used to initialise the executor

    • Method Detail

      • iterator

        public java.util.Iterator<MediaRecord> iterator()
        Returns an iterator over the internal sorted set of MediaFile objects.
        Specified by:
        iterator in interface java.lang.Iterable<MediaRecord>
        Returns:
        an Iterator for navigating the media file set

      • start

        public final void start()
                 throws BatchErrorException
        Throws:
        BatchErrorException

      • getRecordCount

        protected int getRecordCount()
        Returns the total number of image files identified.
        Returns:
        the count of processed images

      • createImageVisitor

        private java.nio.file.FileVisitor<java.nio.file.Path> createImageVisitor()
                                                                  throws BatchErrorException
        Returns a FileVisitor instance to traverse the source directory.

        The visitor analyses each file, extracts metadata segments, and determines the Date Taken time-stamp. Each file is then wrapped in a MediaRecord object and added to the internal set for later processing.

        Returns:
        a configured FileVisitor for processing image files
        Throws:
        BatchErrorException - if the source directory is not a valid directory
